reviews.review_title: Very immersive on the Oculus Go
reviews.review_description:
And that's what VR is all about. Immersion. You won't be too impressed with the graphics but that's perfectly fine. Personally, I'll trade graphics for immersion any time.

Using headphones is a must for optimum experience. Locomotion with game controller is FANTASTIC. Excellent smooth turning. No swivel chair necessary! 

Use game controller if you can but keep Go controller nearby as you might use it to bring up Geiger counter. For some reason, you cannot bring up Geiger counter with game controller. It's not an issue if you're paying attention to sound, as you should be doing. 

Easily five stars on the Go.

And again... NO SWIVEL CHAIR NECESSARY !
The shark always kills me I am not even moving . Wtf.

developer_response.body:
Shark also can ear you! If you are generating bubbles (making sounds) and they are nearby you better hide. Also maybe you should try a different approach/path...
EDIT: Check the later version! I have reduced the overall difficulty.

reviews.review_description: I havent really had the chance to "dive in" deep enough yet, but what little I have done is promising.  Really need to take the time to see what I can do as I have yet to find a way to get passed the first part without getting eaten by the shark.  Listening to the Geiger counter always takes me to open water where there is nowhere to hide.  I am intrigued by the ability to actually walk around for movement control, but with it being such an open world it seems really impractical because the only available space to freely walk around in the real world would be outside and that just opens up too many risks while wearing the headset.  But hopefully I will get to experience more of the game soon, figure out how to sneak passed the shark.

developer_response.body:
Hi Azrael and good luck surviving, figuring out how to get past the sharks is the main "puzzle" of the levels! The geiger counter points to the overall direction, like a compass. You can walk on the spot, no need to move around really! sometimes it is good to keep a hand touching a chair/wall to make sure you do not wander.
Edit: Someone has made a video successfully finishing the first level (and almost the second) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-UDRi2GXFT8 In case you still need help!

reviews.review_description: In vr better.Praise Jesus who saved my soul .

The reviews seem polarizing, and unfortunately I fall on the side of frustrated. Graphics are decent but certainly not best in class as some reviewers bizarrely claim. MAJOR kudos to developer for trying to introduce both motion walking AND breath detection in the same game! Very ambitious and innovative. 

BUT it's all for nothing because the game makes no sense. The first shark ate me every time even with zero movement or breath. After playing 20+ times, I finally beat it just by running fast underneath it, which does not seem how the game is supposed to go. The sharks just follow the same rail path. If you have to look up YouTube vids to beat the very first obstacle, you need a better tutorial or more intuitive/simple gameplay. Would have kept playing based on concept, but too frustrating. The motion walking wasn't consistent, which also caused me to get eaten many times, maybe better with gamepad?

EDIT: I didn't even know you could hide. The instructions imply not moving or making bubbles. Would definitely help to make it more clear that you need to hide and that shark will get you if it sees you even with no movement/bubbles

developer_response.body: Edit: I know this is an old comment but would like to clarify for next users that I rolled some gameplay updates in which the geiger counter points you towards closer hideouts and guides you properly through the level. You still need to hide from the shark but now the experience is much enjoyable. Thanks for your feedback!

reviews.review_description: Not bad at all but yes the waterdrops on the visor are way to close and distracting.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review HommerS66!
EDIT: Version 1.0.1 has been submitted for review and should be live within hours. In this version the drops has been removed until you have less than 8% of oxygen left (pretty much the last breaths") and are reduced in size and number as well anyway so they are not so disruptive. Thanks for your feedback!

Sorry you did not enjoy the slow pace of our game! It is certainly not an action packed experience but a more "realistic" stealth approach where patience is key. Have a look at this video if you need help progressing: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-UDRi2GXFT8&t=581s

reviews.review_title: What am I supposed to do?
reviews.review_description:
Even with tips from the developer and with a couple hours of trying I still failed to finish this game. So make from that what you will. The walking mechanism works good one time and barely at all at other times, so I usually ended up using the controller. The submarine thing seems to be a dead end. It's not 'shiny' like he said it would be and the only thing that happens when I bump into it is a loud banging sound. No shark seems to take any interest at all, and nothing else ever happened, no matter how long I tried, so it seems to be just for show. I did manage to get to the top by walking under the shark, but then I get to a place with an orange balloon and a shark swimming by over my head and I'm stuck. At that point there seems to be nothing else I can do, including leaving the spot I'm in, so I uninstalled this game. I think I've tried enough possibilities by now. I suppose the game is either bugged or unsolvable.

Tested on SM-N950F.

edit: I don't really care about the sub being not shiny, it's just that it doesn't seem to be useful in any way whatsoever.
Thank you for clarifying that that was the end of the game, because that wasn't clear to me at all. The game just continued and my only options seemed to be to die or turn off the game.

developer_response.body:
Edit: I know this is an old comment but would like to clarify for next users that I rolled some gameplay updates in which the geiger counter points you towards closer hideouts and guides you properly through the level. You still need to hide from the shark but now the experience is much enjoyable.  (for some reason this answer got lost?, also related to your non shiny sub maybe an update does not roll out for you?)
Edit2: The balloon works with air and that is the end game (well you have to do something with it, there is an end screen of course ). Thanks for playing and all your feedback anyway!

reviews.review_description: This was challenging and immersive at the same time. I've played most of the games out for the gear vr and this is top notch. I felt like I was really underwater, you guys knocked it out of the park. The sharks were not as repetitive as I expected and some of the trails were hidden very well.  I will definitely recommend this game to my friends! A+
Awesome  game  and graphics..  but bloody hard to get pass the shark lol

developer_response.body: Haha Thanks! Well there is a gameplay video around in youtube with some tips ;-) Best luck!

reviews.review_description: Can’t use the controller to walk. I barely got past the boat at the beginning. Bummer
Immersive underwater scenery. You can even see your footsteps in the ocean sand.

reviews.review_title: Amazing must buy!!! Subnautica on the go
reviews.review_description: Ok so i see mixed reviews on games like thus and i wander why. I have a vive and a psvr as well as a mixed reality and my oculus go amd let me say that this looks as good as some games on my pc vr.. so from an experienced vr enthusiest since day 1 ibsalute u fkr thus superb game and would only ask if u do any future titles thay i be allowed to get beta access if possible. Keep uo the awsome work, this games reminds me of subnautica

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your kind words, it really means a lot :D  A new game is on the way and I will make sure betas are sent

reviews.review_description: Awful graphics and gameplay. no positive aspect.
